content: Hi there! I'm Quinn Young, a geologist working in mining exploration. Just got back from a six-month sabbatical traveling through South America - absolutely incredible experience!
Welcome back, Quinn! That sounds like an amazing adventure. Which countries did you visit during your sabbatical?
I started in Colombia, made my way through Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, Chile, and ended in Argentina. The landscapes were breathtaking - from the Andes to the Atacama Desert.
That's quite the journey! As a geologist, I bet you found the geological features fascinating. Did any particular location stand out?
Absolutely! The salt flats in Bolivia were mind-blowing. I also loved hiking around the volcanic areas in Chile. When I'm not working or traveling, I enjoy origami, archery, and brewing my own beer.
Oh, and I've recently gotten into stand-up comedy - took a class while I was in Buenos Aires. It was terrifying but so much fun!
Wow, you have such diverse interests! How did you get into origami and archery?
I started origami about 10 years ago - it's so meditative. Archery I picked up three years ago at a friend's suggestion. We have a little club that meets every other weekend.
That's great! And brewing your own beer sounds interesting too. What's your favorite style to make?
I love making IPAs - experimenting with different hop combinations is so rewarding. Last batch I made a mango-habanero IPA that turned out surprisingly well!
That sounds delicious! I'd love to try it sometime. How are you adjusting back to work after your sabbatical?
It's been an adjustment, but I'm excited to be back. I actually have a project meeting tomorrow morning to discuss a new copper exploration site in Arizona. Gotta get my sleep schedule sorted!
Sounds like an important meeting. Best of luck with the project! And maybe bring some of that mango-habanero IPA to share with the team?
Ha! Maybe I will. Thanks for the suggestion. I should probably get some rest now though.
Good idea, Quinn. Enjoy your evening and have a great meeting tomorrow!
